rulings.Case Title: MATTHEW MCKINES VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, MAY 20, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 7, Plaintiff's motion for order granting leave to file first amended complaint is granted. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: DONALD L. BASS et al VS. SEQUOIA VENTURES, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, MAY 20, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 1, Defendant Exxon Mobil Corp.'s motion for summary judgment or, in the alternative, summary adjudication: the parties shall appear at 9:45 a.m. to discuss with the Court the likely continuance of this motion. The Court denies defendant's request to exclude Mr. Manwill's declaration but if defendant wishes the Court will continue this hearing to allow defendant to depose Mr. Manwill and submit a supplemental reply. If defendant chooses, a supplemental briefing schedule and new hearing date will be determined at the hearing. If defendant declines, the Court will hear argument on the substance of the motion. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. = (503/TLJ)

ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, MAY 20, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 2, Defendant Ameron International Corporation's motion for summary judgment is off calendar at the moving party's request. (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES DEL PINO VS. 3M COMPANY INDIVIDUALLY AND FKA MINNESOTA,
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Or Tax Defendant Rockwell Automation, Inc.S Costs
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, MAY 20, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 4, Plaintiffs' motion to tax defendant Rockwell Automation, Inc.'s memorandum of costs is granted in part and denied in part. Of the $57,240.91 defendant claimed, $5,655 is taxed and defendant is awarded $51,585.91 as follows: Plaintiffs do not challenge the $2,230 claimed for filing and motion fees. Of the $10,879.68 claimed for deposition costs, plaintiffs only challenge the costs related to the depositions of Cutler-Hammer's PMK and Wolfgang Knauss. Plaintiffs' motion is denied as to these challenges as the costs are explicitly allowed and were incurred, reasonably necessary to the conduct of the litigation rather than merely convenient or beneficial to its preparation, and reasonable in amount. Thus the motion is denied as to the full claim for $10,879.68. As to the $44,131.23 claimed for expert witness costs pursuant to C.C.P. 998, Rockwell's offer of a dismissal without prejudice in exchange for a mutual waiver of costs was reasonable and made in good faith and the court uses is discretion to award some of these costs. Of these costs, plaintiffs only challenge hours related to Bifareti Construction, GradCo and Wolfgang Knauss. As to Bifareti Construction, the recoverable hours are reduced to 30 at an hourly rate of $195 and thus $5,655 is taxed and $5,850 is allowed. As to GradCo and Mr. Knauss, plaintiffs' motion is denied and the claimed costs are awarded. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID SEWELL et al VS. GENERAL ELECTRIC COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder In Defendant Union Carbide Corporations Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Or Dismiss Action On The Grounds Of Forum Non Conveniens
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, MAY 20, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 5, Defendant Honeywell International, Inc.'s joinder in Union Carbide Corp.'s motion to stay or dismiss action on the grounds of forum non conveniens is denied as untimely. Honeywell failed to provide proper notice required by C.C.P. 1005(b) and 1010.6. Plaintiffs' objection based on timeliness is sustained. The denial is without prejudice to Honeywell filing its own motion on proper notice. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
